source_pr.title: *: Remove deprecated streaming
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: ref https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/32679

Problem Summary:

Currently we have quite a few deprecated options with no plan to clean them up. This one is not known to confuse/affect users, but I thought I would take a look at the effort required to clean it up, since any simplification of variables helps usability.

It looks relatively straight forward, but we can reassess if feedback reports otherwise.

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
Covered by existing tests, confirming removal is safe.
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [x] Breaking backward compatibility
Removes functionality from the server which was previously deprecated.

Documentation

- [x] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [x] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
The deprecated system variable `tidb-enable-streaming` has been removed.
```

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select query_time, conn_id from `CLUSTER_SLOW_QUERY` order by time limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows("4.895492 6")) (infoschema/cluster_tables_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select query_time, conn_id from `CLUSTER_SLOW_QUERY` order by time limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows("4.895492 6")) (infoschema/cluster_tables_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
